metadata.title: Russia — the silovik state: Putin's power vertical, the energy champions, the shadow fleet, Wagner->Africa Corps, the GRU/SVR/FSB triad, and the gold drawdown
metadata.status: office-holders, agencies, corporate structures, sanctions, shadow-fleet figures, and reserve data are FACT (public record / Treasury / OSINT / CBR); the 'power vertical / sanctions-evasion resilience' reading is graded; composition guard applied (the intel triad competes, it is not one mind)
metadata.frame: The Russia node already anchored CIPS, the APTs, Crude_Oil, and the frozen-Euroclear assets; this block builds out the full structure. Five layers: (1) the POWER VERTICAL — Putin + the siloviki (security-service elite) who run the state and the state enterprises; (2) the ENERGY champions (Gazprom, Rosneft/Sechin) that fund the state and the war; (3) the SHADOW FLEET that keeps the oil — and the revenue — flowing past Western sanctions; (4) the MILITARY/PMC arm (Wagner -> Africa Corps, with the SVR-run influence operation); (5) the INTELLIGENCE triad (GRU/SVR/FSB) that runs the cyber APTs + hybrid war; plus the GOLD drawdown financing a widening war deficit. Ties to spec-surveillance-cyber-threat-layer (APTs), spec-semiconductor-logistics-standards (shipping chokepoints), macro-gold (the hard-money lens), spec-cross-border-settlement-rails (CIPS), and the planned Ukraine block.

the_power_vertical: Putin (25+ years in power) rebuilt a centralized 'power vertical' staffed by SILOVIKI — men from the security services (KGB/FSB lineage) placed atop the state and its major enterprises. The archetype: Igor Sechin (Putin's longtime KGB-colleague aide) chairing/running Rosneft. The war silenced the 1990s-style independent oligarchs: despite a record billionaire count, they have lost political voice and are dependent on the Kremlin (fear + sanctions + selective enforcement). grade: fact (roles); the 'capture of the oligarchy' reading is well-supported interpretation.
the_energy_champions: State energy funds the state. Gazprom (gas) and Rosneft (oil; Sechin) are the crown jewels; oil-and-gas revenue is the fiscal backbone. The Ukraine war + sanctions cut the pipeline-gas market to Europe, shifting Russia toward SEABORNE crude to Asia (India/China) at a discount — which is what makes the shadow fleet essential. grade: fact.
the_shadow_fleet: The sanctions-evasion engine. A fleet of ~400-600+ ageing tankers under flags of convenience, opaque ownership, and substandard insurance now moves the majority of Russia's seaborne crude beyond Western reach. Despite designations, the number of SANCTIONED tankers still loading Russian oil rose from 44 (Jan 2025) to 143 (Nov 2025); by early 2026 G7-sanctioned tankers carried ~68% of Russian crude exports; Ukraine's catalog lists ~1,337 shadow vessels (Feb 2026). The fleet also degrades the maritime order (uninsured tankers in chokepoints; cable-cutting incidents). grade: fact (the figures); 'sanctions failed' is the supported reading.
the_military_pmc: After Prigozhin's 2023 mutiny and death, Wagner's footprint was absorbed by Africa Corps — a paramilitary fully subordinate to the Russian Defense Ministry — while the SVR (foreign intelligence) quietly took over Wagner's INFLUENCE/disinformation arm ('Africa Politology' / 'The Company', ~100 consultants). Africa Corps operates across the Sahel/Libya (Mali, Burkina Faso, Niger, Libya, CAR), trading security for mining/gold access and political alignment. grade: fact.
the_intelligence_triad: Three rival services: FSB (Bortnikov, since 2008 — domestic security, counterintel, border guard, the most powerful), GRU (Kostyukov, since 2018 — military intel; runs APT28/Fancy Bear + Sandworm), SVR (Naryshkin, since 2016 — foreign intel; runs APT29/Cozy Bear + the Africa influence op). KEY NUANCE (composition guard): they COMPETE more than they cooperate — multiple, sometimes contradictory tracks, not one disciplined strategy. This refines the cyber-threat-layer attributions (GRU/SVR as the specific operators behind the Russia-attributed APTs). grade: fact.
the_gold_drawdown: The fiscal strain shows in the reserves. Facing a budget deficit (~$61B by Mar 2026) the war and oil-discounts widened, the Bank of Russia has been SELLING gold — ~22 tonnes in early 2026, reserves down to ~2,305 tonnes (a multi-year low), with estimates it could sell ~230 tonnes (~$30B) across the year. The 'gold-fortress' built pre-war is being spent down to fund the war — the hard-money lens applied to a sanctioned petrostate. grade: fact (CBR data); the 'fortress being spent' framing is interpretation.
the_honest_reading: Russia is a sanctioned petrostate run by a security-service elite, financing a war through discounted seaborne oil (shadow fleet), a spent-down gold reserve, and alternative settlement rails (CIPS) — while projecting power via a state-PMC (Africa Corps) and a competing-but-active intelligence triad waging hybrid/cyber war. The structural finding is RESILIENCE-VIA-EVASION (sanctions degraded but did not stop the oil revenue) plus FISCAL STRAIN (gold drawdown, deficit). No claim of a single omniscient plan — the siloviki and the intel services are rivalrous (composition guard). Overlay; excluded from the proofs.

sources.name: Baltic Sentinel — Western sanctions failed to curb Russia's shadow fleet in 2025
Wikipedia / Lansing Institute — Africa Corps (Wagner successor)
Kitco — Russia's central bank selling gold to cover budget / ruble
Carnegie — Russia's oligarchs must walk a wartime tightrope
